text.title: pnpm Package Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the pnpm-package-management methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Root package.json MUST contain "packageManager": "pnpm@<major.minor.patch>". Corepack uses this to enforce one version across dev + CI + Docker.
rule.rationale: Lockfile format changes between pnpm major versions; without pinning, different environments produce churn that obscures real dependency changes.

rule.statement: CI MUST run pnpm install --frozen-lockfile. --no-frozen-lockfile is forbidden as a CI workaround.
rule.rationale: --no-frozen-lockfile allows silent drift; reproducible builds require the lockfile to be the source of truth.

rule.statement: shamefully-hoist MUST be false (the default). The only allowed exception is node-linker=hoisted with an inline comment naming the native add-on and linked upstream issue.
rule.rationale: shamefully-hoist=true defeats pnpm's phantom-dependency protection. The real fix for 'module not found' is adding the missing direct dependency.

rule.statement: Root package.json MUST set "scripts.preinstall": "npx -y only-allow pnpm" so accidental npm/yarn invocations fail fast.
rule.rationale: Mixed package managers create a second lockfile, corrupt node_modules layout, and silently break reproducibility.

rule.statement: Internal monorepo dependencies MUST use workspace:* in package.json. pnpm publish rewrites this to a real semver range at publish time.
rule.rationale: workspace:* guarantees the local version is used in dev and the correct semver range is published, with no manual conversion step that agents forget.

rule.statement: pnpm-lock.yaml updates MUST land in a separate commit from application code changes.
rule.rationale: Mixing lockfile + app diffs makes it impossible to bisect which dependency change caused a regression.
